title: Lemonade Stand Change Validator
description:
Determine if At a lemonade stand, each lemonade costs 5. Customers pay with 5, 10, or 20. Initially you have no change. Return True if you can provide correct change to every customer in order.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
- bills ∈ {5,10,20}
explanation:
Intuition
Greedy: always prefer to give change using a $10 when possible for a $20 bill because $10 is harder to compose later. Maintain counts of $5 and $10 bills only.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Keep counters for fives and tens
2. Main Algorithm: For bill=5 inc five; for 10, need one five; for 20, prefer ten+five else three fives
3. Return Result: If any check fails, return False; else True

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(1)

Key Insights
- Use larger denomination first for $20 change
- Only $5 and $10 matter for state

Common Pitfalls
- Using three fives when a ten exists may fail later
- Forgetting to check availability before decrement

testCases.explanation: When lemonade_change(bills=[5,5,5,5]), the expected result is true

hints: Track counts of fives and tens
Reject when change cannot be made
